---
title: "Ch. 1: Where to Search?"
subtitle: 'Identifying relevant places for jobs'
author: Maia L. Southwick
date: "2024-12-19"
format: html
---
### Common Sites

- [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/)
- [Handshake](https://joinhandshake.com/)
- [Indeed](https://www.indeed.com/)

## LinkedIn

Originally a professional networking site, LinkedIn has expanded to cover multiple functionalities. This includes hosting job ads, [learning courses](https://learning.linkedin.com/) (formely Lynda.com), running news articles and more (including puzzles).

There are over 230 million users in the US alone ([LinkedIn, 2024](https://news.linkedin.com/about-us#Statistics)). If you do not already have an account, you will need one in order to browse and apply to roles posted on LinkedIn.

### Signing Up

For this workshop, we will be exploring LinkedIn Jobs. You will need to set-up an account. 

If you haven't set-up one already, you can do it now:

1. Go to [linkedin.com](https://www.linkedin.com/).
2. Click "Join now" under the three sign-in options.
3. Continue to fill out the information requested. You will likely use this account after you complete your undergraduate degree, and so signing up with a personal email is suggested. You can add your university email as a secondary email under Settings >> Sign in & security >> Email addresses.

### Creating a Profile

You can connect with classmates, coworkers, and friends. There's nothing incredibly special about having a large number of connections, especially when it comes to securing a job. The main benefit of having more connections is catching when someone re-posts or posts a job ad. This could be relevant when you're looking at organizations you may want to work at, but they do not have any current openings. 

- Learn some tips for building out your LinkedIn profile! 
  - The UCD Career Center has a [great page for this](https://careercenter.ucdavis.edu/job-and-internship-search/finding-positions/linkedin-and-others).

### Pro-tips

- With your student email, you can sign up for courses on LinkedIn Learning for free (mostly). If you're want to upskill in a specific area, you can check out this courses and add completion of them to your LinkedIn profile. However, be mindful of services that request payment, whether it's on LinkedIn or elsewhere.


## Job Banks

- EdJoin
- Psychology organization job banks
    - APA PsycInfo
    - Listservs LIKE Cog Dev, SDJM, others (ask other grad students) plus their sites
- 

## Other Events

- [Career Fairs via the Career Center](https://careercenter.ucdavis.edu/career-center-services/career-fairs). Steps for preparing for these fairs are listed at the bottom of the page.
  - Winter Career Fair: Wednesday, Feb. 5, 2025 from 11-3p.m.
  - Spring Career Fair: Wednesday, Apr. 16, 2025 from 11-3.pm.
  - Both are at the University Credit Union Center
